articles.headline: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
articles.deck: Nvidia kicks off its GTC conference with Huang's keynote on Monday, which can be watched in person or via livestream.

articles.headline: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
articles.deck: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

articles.headline: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
articles.deck: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

articles.headline: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
articles.deck: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to reengage its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

articles.headline: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
articles.deck: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

articles.headline: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
articles.deck: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

articles.headline: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
articles.deck: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

articles.headline: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
articles.deck: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an example of the kind of

articles.headline: Alexa+ gets a new ‘adults only’ personality option that curses but won’t get into NSFW content
articles.deck: The new Sassy style can curse and roast you, but the fun ends there.

articles.headline: Wonderful raises $150M Series B at $2B valuation
articles.deck: The funding round, led by Insight Partners, comes just four months after Wonderful raised a $100 million Series A.

articles.headline: Google is using old news reports and AI to predict flash floods
articles.deck: A new way to solve data scarcity: Turning qualitative reports into quantitative data with an LLM.

articles.headline: Google Maps is getting an AI ‘Ask Maps’ feature and upgraded ‘immersive’ navigation
articles.deck: The tech giant says the "Immersive Navigation" launch is the biggest update to Maps in over a decade.

articles.headline: AI ‘actor’ Tilly Norwood put out the worst song I’ve ever heard
articles.deck: This song is an AI actor's rallying cry to other AI actors, urging them to keep going despite the naysayers who doubt their humanity. Literally no one can relate to this.

articles.headline: Ford’s new AI assistant will help fleet owners know if seatbelts are being used
articles.deck: Ford Pro AI debuted at Work Truck Week in Indianapolis and is now available to all of its U.S.-based Pro telematics subscribers.

articles.headline: Zendesk acquires agentic customer service startup Forethought
articles.deck: Forethought was years ahead of its time and the 2018 winner of TechCrunch Battlefield.

articles.headline: Netflix may have paid $600 million for Ben Affleck’s AI startup
articles.deck: This deal could rank as among the streaming giant's largest acquisitions ever.

articles.headline: Lovable says it added $100M in revenue last month alone, with just 146 employees
articles.deck: Swedish vibe-coding unicorn Lovable crossed $400 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) in February.

articles.headline: Replit snags $9B valuation 6 months after hitting $3B
articles.deck: Replit raised a new $400 million round and said it hopes to have $1B in ARR by year's end.

articles.headline: WordPress debuts a private workspace that runs in your browser via a new service, my.WordPress.net
articles.deck: WordPress’s new browser-based service lets users create private sites without hosting or signing up, turning the platform into a personal workspace for writing, research, and AI tools.

articles.headline: Rivian spin-out Mind Robotics raises $500M for industrial AI-powered robots
articles.deck: The startup, which was created by Rivian founder RJ Scaringe, is looking to train on data from, and deploy in, Rivian's factory.
